name: napari-plugin-engine

metadata.name: napari-plugin-engine
metadata.summary: napari plugin engine, fork of pluggy

documentation:
# napari plugin engine

[![License](https://img.shields.io/pypi/l/napari-plugin-engine.svg?color=green)](https://github.com/napari/napari-plugin-engine/raw/master/LICENSE)
[![Build Status](https://travis-ci.com/napari/napari-plugin-engine.svg?branch=master)](https://travis-ci.com/napari/napari-plugin-engine)
[![Docs Status](https://readthedocs.org/projects/napari-plugin-engine/badge/?version=latest)](https://readthedocs.org/projects/napari_plugin_engine/)
[![codecov](https://codecov.io/gh/napari/napari/branch/master/graph/badge.svg)](https://codecov.io/gh/napari/napari)
[![Python Version](https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/napari-plugin-engine.svg?color=green)](https://python.org)
[![PyPI](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/napari-plugin-engine.svg?color=green)](https://pypi.org/project/napari-plugin-engine)

`napari-plugin-engine` is a fork of [pluggy](https://github.com/pytest-dev/pluggy),
modified by the [napari](https://github.com/napari/napari) team.

There are some API and feature changes, including:

- discovery via [naming
  convention](https://packaging.python.org/guides/creating-and-discovering-plugins/#using-naming-convention)
  as well as
  [entry_points](https://packaging.python.org/guides/creating-and-discovering-plugins/#using-package-metadata)
- support for reordering of hook calls after registration.
- enhanced API for retrieving plugin package metadata.
- modified plugin call and registration exception handling
- modified `HookResult` object and hook call loop, with ability to retrieve the
  `HookImplementation` responsible for the result.
- lazy plugin discovery
- some changes to variable and attribute naming
- removed all deprecated code
- type annotations on everything
- pytest fixtures for testing
- a couple napari-specific convenience imports

For usage overview and a reference for the `napari-plugin-engine` API, see our
[Documentation](https://napari-plugin-engine.readthedocs.io/en/latest/)

(see also: the [pluggy documentation](https://pluggy.readthedocs.io/en/latest/))

## install

```shell
pip install napari-plugin-engine
```

## Usage

see [documentation](https://napari-plugin-engine.readthedocs.io/en/latest/usage.html)

functions.name: napari_plugin_engine.implementation.varnames
documentation:
Return tuple of positional and keywrord argument names for a function,
method, class or callable.

In case of a class, its `__init__` method is considered.
For methods the `self` parameter is not included.
TODO: can this be improved?

modules.name: napari_plugin_engine.markers
documentation: Hook annotation decorators

classes.name: napari_plugin_engine.markers.HookSpecificationMarker

methods.name: napari_plugin_engine.markers.HookSpecificationMarker.__call__
documentation:
if passed a function, directly sets attributes on the function
which will make it discoverable to
{py:meth}`.PluginManager.add_hookspecs`. If passed no function, returns
a decorator which can be applied to a function later using the
attributes supplied.

If `firstresult` is `True` the 1:N hook call (N being the number of
registered hook implementation functions) will stop at I\<=N when the
I'th function returns a non-`None` result.

If `historic` is `True` calls to a hook will be memorized and
replayed on later registered plugins.

Decorator helper class for marking functions as hook specifications.

You can instantiate it with a project_name to get a decorator. Calling
{py:meth}`.PluginManager.add_hookspecs` later will discover all marked
functions if the {py:class}`.PluginManager` uses the same project_name.

classes.name: napari_plugin_engine.markers.HookImplementationMarker

methods.name: napari_plugin_engine.markers.HookImplementationMarker.__call__
documentation:
Call the marker instance.

If passed a function, directly sets attributes on the function which
will make it discoverable to {meth}`.PluginManager.register`. If passed
no function, returns a decorator which can be applied to a function
later using the attributes supplied.

# Parameters

function

: A function to decorate as a hook implementation, If `function` is
  None, this method returns a function that can be used to decorate
  other functions.

hookwrapper

: Whether this hook implementation behaves as a hookwrapper.
  by default False

optionalhook

: If `True`, a missing matching hook specification will not result
  in an error (by default it is an error if no matching spec is
  found), by default False.

tryfirst

: If `True` this hook implementation will run as early as possible
  in the chain of N hook implementations for a specification, by
  default False

trylast

: If `True` this hook implementation will run as late as possible
  in the chain of N hook implementations, by default False

specname

: If provided, `specname` will be used instead of the function name
  when matching this hook implementation to a hook specification
  during registration, by default the implementation function name
  must match the name of the corresponding hook specification.

# Returns

Callable

: If `function` is not `None`, will decorate the function with
  attributes, and return the function.  If `function` is None, will
  return a decorator that can be used to decorate functions.

Decorator helper class for marking functions as hook implementations.

You can instantiate with a `project_name` to get a decorator. Calling
{meth}`.PluginManager.register` later will discover all marked functions if
the {class}`.PluginManager` uses the same project_name.

# Parameters

project_name

: A namespace for plugin implementations.  Implementations decorated with
  this class will be discovered by `PluginManager.register` if and only
  if `project_name` matches the `project_name` of the
  `PluginManager`.

classes.name: napari_plugin_engine.exceptions.PluginError

methods.name: napari_plugin_engine.exceptions.PluginError.get
documentation:
Return errors that have been logged, filtered by parameters.

# Parameters

manager

: If provided, will restrict errors to those that are owned by
  `manager`.

plugin_name

: If provided, will restrict errors to those that were raised by
  `plugin_name`.

error_type

: If provided, will restrict errors to instances of `error_type`.

# Returns

list of PluginError

: A list of PluginErrors that have been instantiated during this
  session that match the provided parameters.

# Raises

TypeError

: If `error_type` is provided and is not an exception class.

methods.name: napari_plugin_engine.exceptions.PluginError.log
documentation:
Log this error with metadata, optionally provide logger and level.

Parameters
----------
package_info : bool, optional
    If true, will include package metadata in log, by default True
logger : logging.Logger or str, optional
    A Logger instance or name of a logger to use, by default None
level : int, optional
    The logging level to use, by default logging.ERROR

methods.name: napari_plugin_engine.exceptions.PluginError.info
documentation: Return info as would be returned from sys.exc_info().

Base class for exceptions relating to plugins.

# Parameters

message

: An optional error message, by default ''

namespace

: The python object that caused the error, by default None

cause

: Exception that caused the error. Same as `raise * from`.
  by default None

If a hook is called incorrectly.

Usually this results when a HookCaller is called without the appropriate
arguments.

classes.name: napari_plugin_engine.exceptions.PluginCallError

functions.name: napari_plugin_engine._testsupport.add_specification
documentation: Return a decorator that adds a HookSpecification to test_plugin_manager.

functions.name: napari_plugin_engine._testsupport.add_implementation
documentation: Return a decorator that adds a HookImplementation to test_plugin_manager.

functions.name: napari_plugin_engine._testsupport.caller_from_implementation
documentation:
Return hook caller with implementation as its own spec definition.

Adds a specification and implementation to the test_plugin_manager based on
a single function definition (e.g. assumes that the implementation has the
correct signature).  Returns the hook caller instance prepopulated with the
hook implementation.

functions.name: napari_plugin_engine._testsupport.temporary_hookimpl
documentation:
A fixture that can be used to insert a HookImplementation in the hook call loop.

Use as a context manager, which will return the hook_caller for the
corresponding hook specification.

# Example

% code-block: python
%
% def my_hook_implementation(arg):
%     raise ValueError("shoot!")
%
% with temporary_hookimpl(my_hook_implementation) as hook_caller:
%     with pytest.raises(PluginCallError):
%         hook_caller(arg=42)

functions.name: napari_plugin_engine.dist.get_dist
documentation:
Return a {class}`importlib.metadata.Distribution` for any python object.

# Parameters

obj

: A python object. If a string, will be interpreted as a dist name.

# Returns

dist: Distribution

: The distribution object for the corresponding package, if found.

functions.name: napari_plugin_engine.dist.get_metadata
documentation:
Get metadata for this plugin.

Valid arguments are any keys from the Core metadata specifications:
<https://packaging.python.org/specifications/core-metadata/>

# Parameters

args

: (Case insensitive) names of metadata entries to retrieve.

# Returns

str or dict, optional

: If a single argument is provided, the value for that entry is
  returned (which may be `None`).
  If multiple arguments are provided, a dict of {arg: value} is
  returned.

functions.name: napari_plugin_engine.dist.standard_metadata
documentation:
Return a standard metadata dict for `plugin`.

# Parameters

plugin

: A python object.  If a string, will be interpreted as a dist name.

# Returns

metadata

: A  dicts with plugin object metadata. The dict is guaranteed to have
  the following keys:

  - **package**: The name of the package
  - **version**: The version of the plugin package
  - **summary**: A one-line summary of what the distribution does
  - **author**: The author’s name
  - **email**: The author’s (or maintainer's) e-mail address.
  - **license**: The license covering the distribution
  - **url**: The home page for the package, or dowload url if N/A.

# Raises

ValueError

: If no distribution can be found for `plugin`.

modules.name: napari_plugin_engine.callers
documentation: Call loop machinery.

functions.name: napari_plugin_engine.callers._multicall
documentation:
The primary {class}`~napari_plugin_engine.HookImplementation` call loop.

# Parameters

hook_impls

: A sequence of hook implementation (HookImplementation) objects

caller_kwargs

: Keyword:value pairs to pass to each `hook_impl.function`.  Every
  key in the dict must be present in the `argnames` property for each
  `hook_impl` in `hook_impls`.

firstresult

: If `True`, return the first non-null result found, otherwise, return
  a list of results from all hook implementations, by default False

# Returns

outcome

: A {class}`HookResult` object that contains the results returned by
  plugins along with other metadata about the call.

# Raises

HookCallError

: If one or more of the keys in `caller_kwargs` is not present in one
  of the `hook_impl.argnames`.

PluginCallError

: If `firstresult == True` and a plugin raises an Exception.

classes.name: napari_plugin_engine.callers.HookResult

methods.name: napari_plugin_engine.callers.HookResult.from_call
documentation:
Used when hookcall monitoring is enabled.

https://pluggy.readthedocs.io/en/latest/#call-monitoring

methods.name: napari_plugin_engine.callers.HookResult.force_result
documentation:
Force the result(s) to `result`.

This may be used by hookwrappers to alter this result object.

If the hook was marked as a `firstresult` a single value should
be set otherwise set a (modified) list of results. Any exceptions
found during invocation will be deleted.

methods.name: napari_plugin_engine.callers.HookResult.result
documentation:
Return the result(s) for this hook call.

If the hook was marked as a `firstresult` only a single value
will be returned otherwise a list of results.

A class to store/modify results from a {func}`~hooks._multicall` hook loop.

Results are accessed in `.result` property, which will also raise
any exceptions that occured during the hook loop.

# Parameters

results

: A list of (result, HookImplementation) tuples, with the result and
  HookImplementation object responsible for each result collected during
  a \_multicall loop.

excinfo

: The output of sys.exc_info() if raised during the multicall loop.

firstresult

: Whether the hookspec had `firstresult == True`, by default False.
  If True, self.\_result, and self.implementation will be single values,
  otherwise they will be lists.

plugin_errors

: A list of any {class}`PluginCallError` instances that were created
  during the multicall loop.

modules.name: napari_plugin_engine.hooks
documentation: Internal hook annotation, representation and calling machinery.

variables.name: napari_plugin_engine.hooks.HookExecFunc
documentation:
A function that loops calling a list of
{class}`~napari_plugin_engine.HookImplementation` s and returns a
{class}`~napari_plugin_engine.HookResult`.

# Parameters

hook_caller

: a {class}`HookCaller` instance.

hook_impls

: a list of {class}`~napari_plugin_engine.HookImplementation` instances to call.

kwargs

: a mapping of keyword arguments to provide to the implementation.

# Returns

result

: The {class}`~napari_plugin_engine.HookResult` object resulting from the call loop.

classes.name: napari_plugin_engine.hooks.HookCaller

methods.name: napari_plugin_engine.hooks.HookCaller._add_hookimpl
documentation: Add an implementation to the callback chain.

methods.name: napari_plugin_engine.hooks.HookCaller.call_historic
documentation:
Call the hook with given `kwargs` for all registered plugins and
for all plugins which will be registered afterwards.

If `result_callback` is not `None` it will be called for for each
non-`None` result obtained from a hook implementation.

If `with_impl` is `True`, the caller is indicating that
`result_callback` has a signature of `callback(result, hookimpl)`,
and will be called as such.

methods.name: napari_plugin_engine.hooks.HookCaller.call_extra
documentation:
Call the hook with some additional temporarily participating
methods using the specified `kwargs` as call parameters.

methods.name: napari_plugin_engine.hooks.HookCaller._maybe_apply_history
documentation: Apply call history to a new hookimpl if it is marked as historic.

methods.name: napari_plugin_engine.hooks.HookCaller.get_plugin_implementation
documentation: Return hook implementation instance for `plugin_name` if found.

methods.name: napari_plugin_engine.hooks.HookCaller.index
documentation: Return index of plugin_name or a HookImplementation in self._nonwrappers

methods.name: napari_plugin_engine.hooks.HookCaller.bring_to_front
documentation:
Move items in `new_order` to the front of the call order.

By default, hook implementations are called in last-in-first-out order
of registration, and pluggy does not provide a built-in way to
rearrange the call order of hook implementations.

This function accepts a {class}`HookCaller` instance and the desired
`new_order` of the hook implementations (in the form of list of
plugin names, or a list of actual {class}`HookImplementation`
instances) and reorders the implementations in the hook caller
accordingly.

:::{note}
Hook implementations are actually stored in *two* separate list
attributes in the hook caller: {attr}`HookCaller._wrappers` and
{attr}`HookCaller._nonwrappers`, according to whether the
corresponding {class}`HookImplementation` instance was marked as a
wrapper or not. This method *only* sorts \_nonwrappers.
:::

# Parameters

new_order

: instances The desired CALL ORDER of the hook implementations.  The
  list does *not* need to include every hook implementation in
  {meth}`get_hookimpls`, but those that are not included will be left
  at the end of the call order.

# Raises

TypeError

: If any item in `new_order` is neither a string (plugin_name) or a
  `HookImplementation` instance.

ValueError

: If any item in `new_order` is neither the name of a plugin or a
  `HookImplementation` instance that is present in self.\_nonwrappers.

ValueError

: If `new_order` argument has multiple entries for the same
  implementation.

# Examples

Imagine you had a hook specification named `print_plugin_name`, that
expected plugins to simply print their own name. An implementation
might look like:

```pycon
>>> # hook implementation for ``plugin_1``
>>> @hook_implementation
... def print_plugin_name():
...     print("plugin_1")
```

If three different plugins provided hook implementations. An example
call for that hook might look like:

```pycon
>>> plugin_manager.hook.print_plugin_name()
plugin_1
plugin_2
plugin_3
```

If you wanted to rearrange their call order, you could do this:

```pycon
>>> new_order = ["plugin_2", "plugin_3", "plugin_1"]
>>> plugin_manager.hook.print_plugin_name.bring_to_front(new_order)
>>> plugin_manager.hook.print_plugin_name()
plugin_2
plugin_3
plugin_1
```

You can also just specify one or more item to move them to the front
of the call order:
\>>> plugin_manager.hook.print_plugin_name.bring_to_front(\["plugin_3"\])
\>>> plugin_manager.hook.print_plugin_name()
plugin_3
plugin_2
plugin_1

methods.name: napari_plugin_engine.hooks.HookCaller._set_plugin_enabled
documentation:
Enable or disable the hook implementation for a specific plugin.

# Parameters

plugin_name

: The name of a plugin implementing `hook_spec`.

enabled

: Whether or not the implementation should be enabled.

# Raises

KeyError

: If `plugin_name` has not provided a hook implementation for this
  hook specification.

methods.name: napari_plugin_engine.hooks.HookCaller.enable_plugin
documentation: enable implementation for `plugin_name`.

methods.name: napari_plugin_engine.hooks.HookCaller.disable_plugin
documentation: disable implementation for `plugin_name`.

methods.name: napari_plugin_engine.hooks.HookCaller._call_plugin
documentation:
Call the hook implementation for a specific plugin

:::{note}
This method is not intended to be called directly. Instead, just
call the instance directly, specifing the `_plugin` argument.
See the {meth}`__call__` method.
:::

# Parameters

plugin_name

: Name of the plugin

# Returns

Any

: Result of implementation call provided by plugin

# Raises

TypeError

: If the implementation is a hook wrapper (cannot be called directly)

TypeError

: If positional arguments are provided

HookCallError

: If one of the required arguments in the hook specification is not
  present in `kwargs`.

PluginCallError

: If an exception is raised when calling the plugin

methods.name: napari_plugin_engine.hooks.HookCaller.call_with_result_obj
documentation:
Call hook implementation(s) for this spec and return HookResult.

The {class}`HookResult` object carries the result (in its `result`
property) but also additional information about the hook call, such
as the implementation that returned each result and any call errors.

# Parameters

\_skip_impls

: A list of HookImplementation instances that should be *skipped* when calling
  hook implementations, by default None

kwargs

: keys should match the names of arguments in the corresponding hook
  specification, values will be passed as arguments to the hook
  implementations.

# Returns

result

: A {class}`HookResult` object that contains the results returned by
  plugins along with other metadata about the call.

# Raises

HookCallError

: If one or more of the keys in `kwargs` is not present in
  one of the `hook_impl.argnames`.

PluginCallError

: If `firstresult == True` and a plugin raises an Exception.

methods.name: napari_plugin_engine.hooks.HookCaller.__call__
documentation:
Call hook implementation(s) for this spec and return result(s).

This is the primary way to call plugin hook implementations.

:::{note}
Parameters are prefaced by underscores to reduce potential conflicts
with argument names in hook specifications.  There is a test in
{func}`test_hook_specifications.test_annotation_on_hook_specification`
to ensure that these argument names are never used in one of our
hookspecs.
:::

# Parameters

\_plugin

: The name of a specific plugin to use.  By default all
  implementations will be called (though if `firstresult==True`,
  only the first non-None result will be returned).

\_skip_impls

: A list of HookImplementation instances that should be *skipped* when calling
  hook implementations, by default None

kwargs

: keys should match the names of arguments in the corresponding hook
  specification, values will be passed as arguments to the hook
  implementations.

# Raises

HookCallError

: If one or more of the keys in `kwargs` is not present in one of
  the `hook_impl.argnames`.

PluginCallError

: If `firstresult == True` and a plugin raises an Exception.

# Returns

result

: If the hookspec was declared with `firstresult==True`, a single
  result will be returned. Otherwise will return a list of results
  from all hook implementations for this hook caller.

  If `_plugin` is provided, will return the single result from the
  specified plugin.

methods.name: napari_plugin_engine.hooks.HookCaller._check_call_kwargs
documentation:
Warn if any keys in the hookspec are not present in this call.

It's possible to add arguments to hook specifications (as they evolve).
Here we just emit a warning if there are arguments in the hookspec that
were not specified in this call, which may mean the call could be
updated.

The primary hook-calling object.

A {class}`PluginManager` may have multiple `HookCaller` objects and they
are stored in the `plugin_manager.hook` namespace, named after the `hook
specification` that they represent.  For instance:

```python
pm = PluginManager("demo")
pm.add_hookspec(some_module)
# assuming `some_module` had an @hookspec named `my specification`
assert isinstance(pm.hook.my_specification, HookCaller)
```

Each `HookCaller` instance stores all of the {class}`HookImplementation` objects
discovered during {meth}`plugin registration <PluginManager.register>`
(each of which capture the implementation of a specific plugin for this
hook specification).

The `HookCaller` instance also usually creates and stores a reference to
the {class}`HookSpecification` instance that encapsulates information about the hook
specification, (at `HookCaller.spec`)

# Parameters

name

: The name of the `hook specification` that this `HookCaller`
  represents.

hook_execute

: A {data}`.HookExecFunc` function.  In almost every case, this will be
  provided by the {class}`PluginManager` during hook registration as
  {meth}`PluginManager._hookexec`... which is, in turn, mostly just a
  wrapper around {func}`._multicall`.

namespace

: An namespace (such as a module or class) to search during `HookSpecification`
  creation for functions decorated with `@hookspec` named with the
  string `name`.

spec_opts

: keyword arguments to be passed when creating the {class}`HookSpecification`
  instance at `self.spec`.

functions.name: napari_plugin_engine.manager.get_canonical_name
documentation:
Return canonical name for a plugin object.

Note that a plugin may be registered under a different name which was
specified by the caller of {meth}`PluginManager.register(plugin, name)
<.PluginManager.register>`. To obtain the name of a registered plugin
use {meth}`get_name(plugin) <.PluginManager.get_name>` instead.

functions.name: napari_plugin_engine.manager.ensure_namespace
documentation:
Convert a `dict` to an object that provides `getattr`.

# Parameters

obj

: An object, may be a `dict`, or a regular namespace object.

name

: A name to use for the new namespace, if created.  by default 'orphan'

# Returns

type

: A namespace object. If `obj` is a `dict`, creates a new `type`
  named `name`, prepopulated with the key:value pairs from `obj`.
  Otherwise, if `obj` is not a `dict`, will return the original
  `obj`.

# Raises

ValueError

: If `obj` is a `dict` that contains keys that are not valid
  [identifiers](https://docs.python.org/3.3/reference/lexical_analysis.html#identifiers).

functions.name: napari_plugin_engine.manager.temp_path_additions
documentation:
A context manager that temporarily adds `path` to sys.path.

# Parameters

path

: A path or list of paths to add to sys.path

# Yields

sys_path

: The current sys.path for the context.

functions.name: napari_plugin_engine.manager.load
documentation:
Load and return a module or attribute of a module (such as a class).

If only a module is indicated by the value, return that module. Otherwise,
return the named object.

functions.name: napari_plugin_engine.manager.iter_available_plugins
documentation:
Discover modules by both naming convention and entry_points.

1. Using naming convention
   : \<<https://packaging.python.org/guides/creating-and-discovering-plugins/#using-naming-convention>>\`\_:
     modules installed in the environment that follow a naming convention
     (e.g. "napari_plugin"), can be discovered using {mod}`pkgutil`. This also
     enables easy discovery using the PyPI [simple API](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0503/)

2) Using package metadata
   : \<<https://packaging.python.org/guides/creating-and-discovering-plugins/#using-package-metadata>>\`\_:
     packages that declare an [entry_point](https://setuptools.readthedocs.io/en/latest/setuptools.html#dynamic-discovery-of-services-and-plugins)
     in their `setup.py` file that matches the `entry_point` argument
     can be discovered using [importlib.metadata](https://docs.python.org/3/library/importlib.metadata.html).

For background on entry points, see the [Entry Point specification](https://packaging.python.org/specifications/entry-points/).

# Parameters

group

: The entry_point group name to search for

prefix

: Any modules found in sys.path whose names begin with `prefix`
  will be imported and searched for hook implementations.

path

: Path or paths to add to sys.path before importing, removed at the end.

include_uninstalled

: Whether to search for "local" (uninstalled) modules.  Requires that a
  prefix is provided.  By default, True when prefix is provided.

# Raises

ValueError

: If `include_uninstalled` is true and `prefix` is not provided.

classes.name: napari_plugin_engine.manager.PluginManager

methods.name: napari_plugin_engine.manager.PluginManager.hooks
documentation: An alias for PluginManager.hook

methods.name: napari_plugin_engine.manager.PluginManager._hookexec
documentation:
Returns a function that will call a set of hookipmls with a caller.

This function will be passed to `HookCaller` instances that are
created during hookspec and plugin registration.

If {meth}`~.PluginManager.enable_tracing` is used, it will set it's own
wrapper function at self.\_inner_hookexec to enable tracing of hook
calls.

# Parameters

caller

: The HookCaller instance that will call the HookImplementations.

methods

: A list of {class}`~napari_plugin_engine.HookImplementation` objects
  whose functions will be called during the hook call loop.

kwargs

: Keyword arguments to pass when calling the `HookImplementation`.

# Returns

{class}`~napari_plugin_engine.HookResult`

: The result object produced by the multicall loop.

methods.name: napari_plugin_engine.manager.PluginManager.iter_available
documentation:
Iterate over available plugins.

# Parameters

path

: If a string is provided, it is added to `sys.path` (and
  `self.discover_path`) before importing, and removed at the end.

entry_point

: An entry_point group to search for, by default
  `self.discover_entry_point` is used

prefix

: If `provided`, modules in the environment starting with
  `prefix` will be imported and searched for hook implementations
  by default `self.discover_prefix` is used

See docstring of {func}`iter_available_plugins` for details.

methods.name: napari_plugin_engine.manager.PluginManager.discover
documentation:
Discover and load plugins.

# Parameters

path

: If a string is provided, it is added to `sys.path` (and
  `self.discover_path`) before importing, and removed at the end.

entry_point

: An entry_point group to search for, by default
  `self.discover_entry_point` is used

prefix

: If `provided`, modules in the environment starting with
  `prefix` will be imported and searched for hook implementations
  by default `self.discover_prefix` is used

ignore_errors

: If `True`, errors will be gathered and returned at the end.
  Otherwise, they will be raised immediately. by default True

# Returns

(count, errs)

: The number of succefully loaded modules, and a list of errors that
  occurred (if `ignore_errors` was `True`)

methods.name: napari_plugin_engine.manager.PluginManager.discovery_blocked
documentation: A context manager that temporarily blocks discovery of new plugins.

methods.name: napari_plugin_engine.manager.PluginManager._load_and_register
documentation:
A helper function to import and register a module as `plugin_name`.

# Parameters

mod

: The name of a module (or class in a module) to load.

plugin_name

: Optional name for plugin, by default `get_canonical_name(plugin)`

# Returns

str or None

: canonical plugin name, or `None` if the name is blocked from
  registering.

# Raises

PluginImportError

: If an exception is raised when importing the module.

PluginRegistrationError

: If an entry_point is declared that is neither a module nor a class.

PluginRegistrationError

: If an exception is raised during plugin registration.

methods.name: napari_plugin_engine.manager.PluginManager.register
documentation:
Register a plugin and return its canonical name or `None`.

# Parameters

plugin

: The namespace (class, module, dict, etc...) to register

name

: Optional name for plugin, by default `get_canonical_name(plugin)`

# Returns

str or None

: canonical plugin name, or `None` if the name is blocked from
  registering.

# Raises

TypeError

: If `namespace` is a string.

ValueError

: if the plugin `name` or `namespace` is already registered.

methods.name: napari_plugin_engine.manager.PluginManager._register_dict
documentation:
Register a dict as a mapping of method name -> method.

# Parameters

dct

: Mapping of method name to method.

name

: The plugin_name to assign to this object, by default None

# Returns

str or None

: canonical plugin name, or `None` if the name is blocked from
  registering.

methods.name: napari_plugin_engine.manager.PluginManager.get_name
documentation: Return name for registered plugin or `None` if not registered.

methods.name: napari_plugin_engine.manager.PluginManager._ensure_plugin
documentation:
Return plugin object given a name or object. Or raise an exception.

Parameters
----------
name_or_object : Any
    Either a string (in which case it is interpreted as a plugin name),
    or a non-string object (in which case it is assumed to be a plugin
    module or class).

Returns
-------
Any
    The plugin object, if found.

Raises
------
KeyError
    If the plugin does not exist.

methods.name: napari_plugin_engine.manager.PluginManager.unregister
documentation:
Unregister a plugin object or `plugin_name`.

# Parameters

name_or_object

: A module/class object or a plugin name (string).

# Returns

module

: The module object, or None if the `name_or_object` was not found.

methods.name: napari_plugin_engine.manager.PluginManager.prune
documentation:
Unregister modules that can no longer be imported.

Useful if pip uninstall has been run during the session.

methods.name: napari_plugin_engine.manager.PluginManager.add_hookspecs
documentation:
Add new hook specifications defined in the given `namespace`.

Functions are recognized if they have been decorated accordingly.

methods.name: napari_plugin_engine.manager.PluginManager.is_registered
documentation: Return `True` if the plugin is already registered.

methods.name: napari_plugin_engine.manager.PluginManager.is_blocked
documentation: Return `True` if the given plugin name is blocked.

methods.name: napari_plugin_engine.manager.PluginManager.set_blocked
documentation:
Block registrations of `plugin_name`, unregister if registered.

# Parameters

plugin_name

: A plugin name to block.

blocked

: Whether to block the plugin.  If `False` will "unblock"
  `plugin_name`.  by default True

methods.name: napari_plugin_engine.manager.PluginManager.get_errors
documentation:
Return a list of PluginErrors associated with `plugin`.

# Parameters

plugin

: If provided, will restrict errors to those that were raised by
  `plugin`.  If a string is provided, it will be interpreted as the
  name of the plugin, otherwise it is assumed to be the actual plugin
  object itself.

error_type

: If provided, will restrict errors to instances of `error_type`.

methods.name: napari_plugin_engine.manager.PluginManager._verify_hook
documentation:
Check validity of a `hookimpl`

# Parameters

hook_caller

: A {class}`HookCaller` instance.

hookimpl

: A {class}`HookImplementation` instance, implementing the hook in
  `hook_caller`.

# Raises

PluginValidationError

: If hook_caller is historic and the hookimpl is a hookwrapper.

PluginValidationError

: If there are any argument names in the `hookimpl` that are not
  in the `hook_caller.spec`.

# Warns

Warning

: If the hookspec has `warn_on_impl` flag (usually a deprecation).

methods.name: napari_plugin_engine.manager.PluginManager.check_pending
documentation:
Make sure all hooks have a specification, or are optional.

# Raises

PluginValidationError

: If a hook implementation that was *not* marked as `optionalhook`
  has been registered for a non-existent hook specification.

methods.name: napari_plugin_engine.manager.PluginManager.get_hookcallers
documentation: get all hook callers for the specified plugin.

methods.name: napari_plugin_engine.manager.PluginManager.add_hookcall_monitoring
documentation:
Add before/after tracing functions for all hooks.

return an undo function which, when called, will remove the added
tracers.

`before(hook_name, hook_impls, kwargs)` will be called ahead of all
hook calls and receive a hookcaller instance, a list of HookImplementation
instances and the keyword arguments for the hook call.

`after(outcome, hook_name, hook_impls, kwargs)` receives the same
arguments as `before` but also a
{py:class}`napari_plugin_engine.callers._Result` object which
represents the result of the overall hook call.

methods.name: napari_plugin_engine.manager.PluginManager.enable_tracing
documentation: Enable tracing of hook calls and return an undo function.

methods.name: napari_plugin_engine.manager.PluginManager.get_metadata
documentation:
Return metadata values for a given plugin

Parameters
----------
plugin : Any
    Either a string (in which case it is interpreted as a plugin name),
    or a non-string object (in which case it is assumed to be a plugin
    module or class).
*values : str
    key(s) to lookup in the plugin object distribution metadata.  At
    least one value must be supplied.

Raises
------
TypeError
    If no values are supplied.
KeyError
    If the plugin does not exist.

methods.name: napari_plugin_engine.manager.PluginManager.get_standard_metadata
documentation:
Return a standard metadata dict for `plugin`.

# Parameters

plugin

: A plugin name or any object.  If it is a plugin name, it
  *must* be a registered plugin.

# Returns

metadata

: A  dicts with plugin metadata. The dict is guaranteed to have the
  following keys:

  - **plugin_name**: The name of the plugin as registered
  - **package**: The name of the package
  - **version**: The version of the plugin package
  - **summary**: A one-line summary of what the distribution does
  - **author**: The author’s name
  - **email**: The author’s (or maintainer's) e-mail address.
  - **license**: The license covering the distribution
  - **url**: The home page for the package, or dowload url if N/A.
  - **hooks**: A list of hookspec names that this plugin implements.

# Raises

KeyError

: If `plugin` is a string, but is not a registered plugin_name.

methods.name: napari_plugin_engine.manager.PluginManager.list_plugin_metadata
documentation:
Return list of standard metadata dicts for every registered plugin.

# Returns

metadata

: A list of dicts with plugin metadata. Every dict in the list is
  guaranteed to have the following keys mentioned in
  {meth}`~PluginManager.get_standard_metadata`

Core class which manages registration of plugin objects and hook calls.

You can register new hooks by calling {meth}`~PluginManager.add_hookspecs`.
You can register plugin objects (which contain hooks) by calling
{meth}`~PluginManager.register`.  The `PluginManager` is initialized with
a `project_name` that is used when discovering *hook specifications* and
*hook implementations*.

For debugging purposes you may call {meth}`.PluginManager.enable_tracing`
which will subsequently send debug information to the trace helper.

# Parameters

project_name

: The name of the host project.  All {class}`HookImplementationMarker`
  and {class}`HookSpecificationMarker` instances must be created using
  the same `project_name` to be detected by this plugin manager.

discover_entry_point

: The default entry_point group to search when discovering plugins with
  {meth}`PluginManager.discover`, by default None

discover_prefix

: The default module prefix to use when discovering plugins with
  {meth}`PluginManager.discover`, by default None

discover_path

: A path or paths to include when discovering plugins with
  {meth}`PluginManager.discover`, by default None

# Examples

```python
from napari_plugin_engine import PluginManager
import my_hookspecs

plugin_manager = PluginManager(
    'my_project',
    discover_entry_point='app.plugin',
    discover_prefix='app_',
)
plugin_manager.add_hookspecs(my_hookspecs)
plugin_manager.discover()

# hooks now live in plugin_manager.hook
# plugin dict is at plugin_manager.plugins
```

classes.name: napari_plugin_engine.manager._HookRelay

methods.name: napari_plugin_engine.manager._HookRelay.__getattribute__
documentation: Trigger manager plugin discovery when accessing hook first time.

methods.name: napari_plugin_engine.manager._HookRelay.items
documentation: Iterate through hookcallers, removing private attributes.

methods.name: napari_plugin_engine.manager._HookRelay.values
documentation: Iterate through hookcallers, removing private attributes.

Hook holder object for storing HookCaller instances.

This object triggers (lazy) discovery of plugins as follows:  When a plugin
hook is accessed (e.g. plugin_manager.hook.napari_get_reader), if
`self._needs_discovery` is True, then it will trigger autodiscovery on
the parent plugin_manager. Note that `PluginManager.__init__` sets
`self.hook._needs_discovery = True` *after* hook_specifications and
builtins have been discovered, but before external plugins are loaded.
